title: It really is a great water bottle! (just make sure to clean it)
text:
I’m so happy with this water bottle. I take it everywhere with me (I try to stay hydrated throughout the day). I fill it up with cold water
in the morning and add ice. It always stays cold for at least 24 hours. The ice does melt but very slowly. And for condensation…well, there really isn’t any.
It’s always dry no matter how much cold water I have inside.
The sport cap works great too. You open it up and there’s a straw. It doesn’t leak too too which is amazing. I actually tested it out by lying
the bottle on its side for 30 minutes. No water came out. So I don’t have to worry about throwing it in my bag when I’m in a rush. I don’t use the
stainless steel cap as often but it didn’t give me any trouble the few times I used it.
Washing is simple as long as you use [[ASIN:B07G4ZG7L4 a bottle brush]] (you can’t fit your hand into it otherwise). All I drink is water so I clean
it every few days. Hot water, dish soap and some vinegar in the sink is all you need. The lid, I do take it off so I can wash it separately.
The whole thing is also dishwasher safe if you prefer that.
The only negative for me is that it doesn’t fit in standard cup holders. For reference, I have 40 oz. Luckily I don’t use the car much nowadays
so it isn’t that big of an issue but I thought I’d mention that in this review. The 24 oz from when I’ve read does fit however so maybe get that
if you want to make use of the cupholders.
So overall it was a great buy. I love it and would recommend to anyone. There are several vibrant colors to choose from too which is always a nice bonus.
_gate_reason: The review mentions several vibrant colors to choose from.

title: Keeps ice longer than 36 hours - 40oz version
text:
I absolutely love this water bottle! It goes everywhere with me - I'm never away from ice cold water. When filling the bottle to the top with ice and adding water, the water stays cold for at least 36 hours. With the 40 oz bottle, the ice melts at a rate of about 1/3 every 12 hours. I don't use the steel lid much, but it does work well the few times I have used it.

The sport lid with the flip up straw works great! It doesn't leak. I tested it when I first received the bottle by filling it with ice and water, then laid it next to me on my bed and left it for an hour. The sport lid isn't as well insulated as the steel lid, so condensation formed on the lid. But the bottle definitely DID NOT leak. This was with the straw closed, of course. Generally, even if the straw is open and the bottle tips, it will not leak. This is because the air hole will be blocked with the water, preventing the water from leaking out of the straw.

The only negative about the 40oz bottle is it doesn't fit in the cup holder of my Chevy Malibu or my Veracruz. However, I just lay the bottle down on the passenger seat. A small amount of condensation might form on the lid after my 40 minute commute, but I also live in an area with > 80% humidity. I accepted the risk that the bottle might not fit in the cup holder - it's worth it for the 40 oz capacity. It sits on TOP of the cup holder/center console without problem, if necessary.

I purchased 18oz bottles for each of my kids to carry around with them. One of my sons will only drink water if it is ICE cold. Other cups he would use would only keep the water cold for about 3 hours. He's 5 and sips water throughout the day. He likes that he can have ice water all day and doesn't need to wait for me to add ice throughout the day. The 18 oz and another 22 oz bottle I purchased all fit easily in the cup holders and are easier for the kids to handle.

In total, we purchased 5 of these - the 40oz, 3 18oz and 1 22oz. Super happy; worth every penny; love the finish on the bottle.

I recommend a silicon sleeve. It will help protect against drops, which haven't happened yet... but with 3 kids carrying these around, they are going to bounce around...
_gate_reason: The review mentions the finish on the bottle and its size.

title: You get what you pay for
text:
I was in need of an insulated metal water bottle as I was scheduled to attend desert warfare training in the Mojave Desert (29 Palms, CA). I had used a Hydroflask in the past, and was impressed with its ability to keep my water cold despite the 105+ degree temperatures during the day. Unfortunately, I had dropped that Hydroflask one too many times, and it seemed to have lost it’s ability to keep liquids cold (or hot). I didn’t want to shell out $40 for another one so I figured I would give the Hydro Cell a try.

It looked and felt like a Hydroflask so I thought I got a good deal. However, I immediately noticed that while it kept my ice water cold, the ice would melt in a few hours, whereas if you put ice and water in a Hydroflask at night, the ice would be there in the morning. I think the problem was the metal lid, which was always cold to the touch even though the bottle itself never got cold, which leads me to believe that the cold was being diffused by the lid.

One more thing that really bothered me was the lid. I haven’t figured out what the problem or the cause was, but there were often times when I would try to unscrew the lid, and it would not come off, no matter how many times I turned/twisted the lid. It was bewilderingly frustrating especially when I was hot and thirsty and wanted to chug some ice water.

I ultimately ended up buying another Hydroflask. Lesson learned.
_gate_reason: The review describes the appearance and feel of the water bottle.

title: Do yourself a favor, and buy this PRONTO!
text:
I am the first to admit that I am HORRIBLE at drinking water, so I had to find a solution.
I’ve always wanted to invest in a hydro flask, but found it too expensive. I’ve purchased water bottles here and there, but it never really kept my water ICE cold and often times it wouldn’t look all that nice to carry around. So when I saw these bottles were half the price of the competitor, I jumped the gun and bought myself the bright orange one in the 40oz! Shipping was super fast through Amazon Prime, and upon arrival, it was packaged nicely. It comes with two caps - a flat cover and straw cap (love this).

The day I received it, I filled it all the way to the top with ice then added water. After 48 hours, I still had little bits of ice in the bottle and the water was still ice cold!!!
I did notice though that with the straw cap that it tends to leak a little if you tilt it all the way over. It was nothing serious, but I reached out to customer service and they were unbelievable! They got back to me on the same day, and sent me the tracking number to a new cap the following day. Talk about PREMIUM CUSTOMER SERVICE!!!

Excellent customer service, fast shipping, stylish/lively color, comes with two caps, quality bottle, no sweating, water stays ice cold for DAYS…Not sure what else I can say, and I’m sure the biggest compliment I can give to this company would be to purchase more in the future and recommend them to all my family and friends…which I will certainly do! Thank you for helping me stay hydrated!
_gate_reason: The review describes the bright orange color and stylish appearance of the bottle.

title: I don't throw five stars around but...
text:
...this is worthy of five stars all around. My family and I live part of the year now in the desert of Nevada and I'll be honest, I was not fully prepared for the heat. My kids weren't drinking enough water and I was sick of seeing plastic water bottles half empty and everywhere. I was also not happy about the fact that I was buying plastic reusable bottles like every month or so. I started noticing that a lot of people around me were using these thermos like water bottles so I looked into them. There's a well know company that is known for these great bottles but honestly $40+ per bottle for a family of five sent me into a small panic. Underneath that well known brand was this brand. The colors were cool, they were half the price, and came with two different lids. They looked identical so I took a chance.

We have had these bottles for exactly 33 days now and this is what I'm doing cartwheels over.

- My kids, without me reminding them constantly, make sure to have it with them wherever we go.
- All five of us drink way more water now.
- I have yet to deal with any type of leak. Not one.
- Everyone is happy with their chosen color and they fit (24 oz size) in car booster seat holders and the cars cup holders.

Obviously, drinking nice ice cold water is way more refreshing than drinking hot water and bottled water here gets HOT quickly. We put ice in our bottles and, no joke, there's still ice when we wake up the next day! Maybe I've been living under a rock just now finding this exists and I don't have to carry a huge thermos around, but I'm sure someone reading this is under that same rock, too. Hopefully you love these bottles as much as we do.

Thanks for coming to my Ted talk.
_gate_reason: The review describes the colors and size of the water bottles.

title: Great water bottle
text:
I've been using this water bottle for a few weeks now and really like it. Though I have other steel water bottles, I find myself reaching for this one every time.
There are several things about it that makes it my go-to bottle:
- The outside of the bottle has a good feel to it. It has just enough texture to make it grippy, without being rough to the touch. And, I like the color.
- It keeps drinks really cold and the ice doesn't melt, which I really like. I've left the bottle in the fridge overnight and the ice in the drink hasn't melted at all.
- It has two separate lids. One is a screw-on lid connected with a rubber attachment that goes around the opening. The rubber is fairly thick and stiff, but it seems to be getting more pliable with use. Something to note, the first time I used this lid, it didn't seal completely. But after I screwed it on really tightly, then opened it and screwed it on normally it sealed just fine. (I'm wondering if the rubber hadn't quite been pushed down all the way and screwing it on tightly pushed it down all the way.) Anyway, it seals fine now.
- The second lid has a spout with a plastic straw that attaches to the underside of the lid. They include a spare straw, which is a nice touch. The thing I really like about the spout lid is the handle you use to carry it around. The ring has smooth rounded edges, rather than square edges, so it's super comfortable to carry.
- Something that might seem unimportant but I think it matters is how it was packaged. The box was sturdy and the bottle and attachments were all padded and protected. Seems like so many times stuff comes with flimsy boxing and no padding, so whatever is inside gets damaged. This was really sturdy.
So, all in all this is a really nice water bottle, and I'll be buying another one soon.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's texture, color, and lid features.

title: Just as good as a HYDRO FLASK!!!
text:
This is the perfect product if you want a Hydro Flask, but don’t want to shell out the money.

I bought this as a secondary water bottle to take traveling, so I wouldn’t worry if it was damaged. All I have to say is damn… This is just as good as a Hydro Flask!

It looks and feels like a Hydro Flask. The texture is nice and matte. The color is nice. It came with a straw lid (which I personally love). Washing is simple and the inside doesn’t hold tastes. I’ve used this to take juice and margaritas to the beach, after a cleaning and refilling with water, it didn’t have any leftover flavor which is huge in my book.

Ice says intact and unmelted. I fill it up with ice water the night before. When I wake up in the morning, its as fresh as when I filled it. Ice cold and ready to start the day. I haven’t tested how long Ice will stay, but from what I’ve seen, it should live up to its promise.
_gate_reason: The review describes the product's texture, color, and appearance.

title: Great water bottle for kids at school
text: I bought these for the kids returning back to school. They were in need of new water bottles this year and I wanted something that was going to keep their drinks cold and not get condensation all over the place. There are several colors to pick through. Because I was buying 4 at one time, I went through the list and found all the colors that were the cheapest at the time so that I could keep my cost down. So, I ended up buying teal, navy, black, and white. I was also drawn into how many reviews these have and how they are highly rated. I am not disappointed. The kids cups get filled up around 7:15 and when they come home after 4, some of them still have ice in them depending on if they refilled their bottles or not. They do not collect any condensation on the outside so nothing ever gets wet when they carry them around or sit them on their desks. They all came with 2 lids. The one lid unscrews and you can drink it. The other is a straw lid that pops up to drink. I can see how the screw lid holds more of a vacuum, but we use the straw lid every day so we don't have to worry about spilling if they had to unscrew the lid everytime they wanted a drink. I don't think the straw lid causes much of a difference in keeping the liquids inside cold. The cups arrived in a box that was nicely packed and the top of each box had a dot of which color was inside. The cups are nice and heavy. They do not feel cheap at all. The one thing that has happened is that some of the color has chipped a little on a couple of the water bottles. This isn't a huge concern of mine because with kids taking them to school everyday I would expect them to get some wear and tear on them. The color chipping a little isn't ruining the integrity of the water bottle of how it holds up to what it is made for. The other thing is they are not dishwasher safe. I don't know why I thought they were, I must have misread that. Obviously, not a deal breaker because most aren't, but would be convenient to be able to throw them in there. So we hand wash all week and then on the weekends I do a vinegar soak for a deeper clean. I am also pleased with the size. They are big enough to hold a good amount of water without being too big and heavy. I chose to get the 18 ounce ones so when they refill at the water bottle fountain it stops and they have to refill a little bit more into the bottle since a standard water bottle is normally 16 ounces. All in all, I am very happy with this purchase and I think these will be used for many years.
_gate_reason: The review describes colors, size, and appearance of the water bottles.

title: When your Hydrocell can't get any better....IT DOES!!!
text:
When I was contacted by Hydrocell to try their new sport mouth bottle, I was SO EXCITED. Even better, that it was at a discount! I own 7 Hydrocells between our family. We've lost two and without hesitation I immediately bought a replacement. I've been wanting to buy a new 40 oz bottle because I purchased my first one back on 7/26/2018 and it has permanently become one of my limbs. I do not go ANYWHERE without it. I have filled up the bottle with ice and water, and left it in the sun on the HOTTEST DAY all day, to still find cold water and sometimes even small bits of melted ice in there. With heavy daily use for almost 2 years and a lot of drops and dents, the bottle never lost it's coldness. I actually couldn't justify buying a new bottle because outside of the dents my old one worked perfectly fine as it did the first day.

One thing I do have to admit with their wide-mouth bottles is that I never used the twist-off cap. It was too hard for me to drink without spilling all over myself. Especially with the kids, it would feel like an unwanted ice cold bath. But, with the sport mouth bottle I find myself CHUGGING it which is great when you are really looking to take down some water post volleyball session, hiking, etc.

The few cons that I have are hardly cons at all:
- The 32 oz does not fit in a standard cup holder (but I purchased it knowing that)
- The screw on cap I feel is MAYBE one turn too much. (I know, I'm practically looking for things wrong with it at this point and it's hard to find). I say this because I ate something really spicy and I felt like it took so many turns just to get the cap on or off.

PROS:
- I LOOOOOOOOOOVE the color of my bottle
- The texture of the bottle. It makes it less slippery.
- The sport mouth so it's easier to drink from without the straw cap
- It keeps my beverage cold for 24 hrs+
- It's great for the pool because it keeps your drink COLD, you can't tell what's inside and it leak-proof :)
- Extremely durable. (I've taken my bottle to the motocross track, volleyball tournaments, on a boat in Havasu, camping, and I've dropped it probably 50x's)
- Hydrocell have one of the best customer service I have ever experienced
_gate_reason: The review describes the color and texture of the bottle.

title: Lid is hit or miss
text:
I received a fuchsia 40oz for Christmas and loved it! I put ice in with a drink and the ice lasted 48 hours. Probably would last longer but that was when I dumped it to wash it. Because I loved it so I ordered another. This one being a red/orange 32oz(40oz was sold out in red/orange at the time). My second one while also keeps ice for a long time the lid/straw isn't great. When I try to drink from it I take in more air bubbles than liquid. That is why I took a star away. Lack of consistency. If I could be guaranteed to get another like my first one I would order another one and then just switch out the lid/straw between the three.

Update: So I contacted customer service about the issue with the lid and straw on my 32oz bottle. They responded in 24 hours, new lid was sent out within 48 hours, and new lid/straw arrived at the end of the week. New lid/straw works perfectly.

Great product and fast, polite customer service.
_gate_reason: Describes colors and sizes of the bottles received.

title: Great COLOR, great size, great quality & price!
text: I am that person that brings a huge jug of water with me everywhere I go. I work from home, so I drink from home, but I’m also active and love to travel. Living in Florida means water is essential every day, and everywhere. Having a bottle I’m in love with helps me keep up with my habit, and so far this bottle has kept me company everywhere I go, even around the house. I have several water bottles but I needed something BIGGER, so the 64 oz option with Hydrocell is perfect. Even when full, the bottle is comfortable to carry with the handled lid it comes with. It’s big, but still comfortable to hold and drink from. The best part, is the color. I’m big on aesthetics and spent a lot of time shopping online for a new water bottle because it couldn’t be boring or basic. It needed to be bright and it needed to be orange. I had a hard time deciding between the deeper orange and the orange/yellow ombre because they’re both gorgeous. I went with the orange/yellow bottle and it makes me smile every time I look at it! Easy to identify I know if I accidentally leave it somewhere (which sometimes I do) no one will get away with stealing it because it’s easy to identify as mine (adding stickers helps too). The value is also really great - a good price for a big, sturdy bottle that so far is leak proof and keeps water nice and cold. Superb quality for a fraction of the price of the big name brands everyone thinks they have to have. I love Hydrocell and eventually hope to buy some more colors to add to my collection… this company does not disappoint! 🌈
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's color, size, and aesthetic appeal.

title: Great bottle, amazing customer service!
text: I ordered the 32oz white and pink bottle and am so happy I did. It keeps my drinks colder than any metal water bottle I have ever had(there's been like 3 or 5) and I really like the sport straw cap. There was a small problem with the lid I got with the bottle dribbling a little and I contacted customer service who immediately asked for my address so they could send a replacement no questions asked. They responded within a few hours and even sent me a tracking number for the replacement lid! I had mentioned to customer service that this was a bigger bottle then I was used to and was having a little harder time handling it due to my chronic health conditions and they offered to send me a Paracord handle for free to help. Highly highly recommend hydro cell! Their customer service has been great to deal with and it is so wonderful to find a company that stands by their product and truly wants customers to be happy. I also like that I have the option to buy replacement caps in different colors if I want to change things up.

title: This is my all-time favorite water bottle!
text:

title: Love it!
text:
I got the 40oz because I wanted something bigger (and less expensive) than my 24oz Hydroflask for days when I'm out and about - especially in the summer heat. The description says it keeps things cold for 18 hours and hot for 8 hours. I tested the cold part recently. The instructions recommend that you fill it with 3/4 ice for maximum effectiveness. I'd say I filled it about 1/2 way with ice and the rest with water. Surprisingly, the ice didn't melt until a little over 24 hours later - and that included refilling the water (I deliberately didn't add more ice). I don't drink hot stuff much, so I doubt I'll be testing that part any time soon. Maybe during winter.

I love that you get the sports cap with it. With Hydroflask that would have been an additional purchase. Also love the colour options. Hydro Cell is definitely worth the money so far. Can't speak to durability as yet, but based on other reviews I figure I better try not to drop it since it probably dents easily (same as my Hydroflask, which I've already dropped and dented because I'm clumsy).
_gate_reason: The review mentions color options and size comparison with another product.

The customer service team reached back to me after 24 hrs and and send out a newer model replacement with additional metal screw cap. The newer model works perfectly and my little one could actually open and close with ease. I attached another photo comparing the two caps(left new right old). The new one bends down nicely resting on the bottle vs flinging backwards pulling on the bottle. And with the additional cap, I now have two perfectly working bottle! Still not happy receiving an older model first but overall satisfied with their customer service team and understands why all the good reviews here.

Original review:
I searched for a long time and finally decided on this one (14oz) because of so many good reviews. Even though it seems to function as advertised, there are three things that bothered me when I got it today. Firstly, the metal lid is pretty hard to close because the treads won’t lineup on the first try….2nd…or 5th time! I did figured out the trick to turn left(loosen) first then right(tighten) and that worked out. Now I just have to teach that to my 4 year old. Secondly, the bottle tips over easily when leave the metal lid open, especially when the bottle is empty or nearly empty. I know this is due to the heavy metal lid pulling it over. If decides to keep it, I will have to heat up the plastic strap to bend/mold it so it won’t fly backwards as much when left open. Thirdly, the hole for the tiny silicon plug have tiny threads of unrefined loose plastic. That came to my attention when found tiny black tread like stuff on the plug. I attached a picture because it’s hard to explain. It’s not a hard fix…just need to file it down but still troublesome. I’m still waiting to compare with others before deciding to keep it or not.

title: Perfect straw lid
text:
This is the fourth Hydro Cell we've purchased. We've gone through a lot of brands through the years, as stainless steel water bottles do have a shelf life--eventually, they've been dropped and dinged enough to compromise the functional integrity and simply need to be replaced. This is probably the best brand I've ever owned.

Pictured is the 32oz size. The body is light by solid, well-constructed, aesthetically pleasing. The finish is powder coated. The clincher for me is the straw lid. (It also comes with a second lid, a screw off cap.) It doesn't have that spongey silicone layer to it, which means it doesn't get mildewy and smelly. The handle is great, sturdy; I can fit 1-2 fingers (female, medium hands) through the loop or a strap of some kind.

We also own 18oz bottles, which come with the flip-top lid--perfect for using as a to-go coffee mug...or flask for pool days ;) It is also a great size for my elementary kids, who use the 18oz for their school water bottle. It keeps their water cold without being too heavy, large, or cumbersome and it fits in their backpack side pocket.
_gate_reason: Describes the bottle's size, finish, and construction details.

title: Absolutely Amazing!
text: I have purchased 3 of these bottles in the past. One 24oz in army green, 40oz in teal, and now the 64oz in army green. This bottle is amazing and holds up to everyday use as well as intensive hikes. I’ve used in hiking the Adirondacks, and in Mexico hiking the desert and it’s allowed me to carry enough water to not have to worry about it all day. It stays cold for a very long time, and I’ve never had any trouble with any of them leaking before. This bottle is the quality of the higher end brands at a much more reasonable price, and overall I’d recommend it to anyone looking for a decent water bottle. You won’t be disappointed with these! The quality is outstanding.

title: Best water bottle I've ever owned
text: I couldn't love this more.  So glad I took a chance.  I bought the 40oz bottle to replace a plastic thermos I was taking water to work in (leaked and didn't keep water cold).  I have been using the Hydro Cell bottle every day for several weeks.  It does not leak - not a drop.  I typically use the straw lid, but the other lid did not leak, either.  It's easy to drink from and it keeps my water cold all day as long as I use ice and cold water.  I filled the bottle on Friday morning, had it out on the counter all day and over the weekend and it still had ice in it on Sunday night.  I fill it about half full with ice, then add cold water.  The outside is a slightly rough material that helps it not slip from my hands.  The 40 oz bottle is it's a little tough to pour from, but easily solved by just using the straw lid.  It has a standard water bottle opening (not wide mouth), so it's a little tough to dispense ice right into it from the automatic ice maker.  I just fill a measuring cup with ice and then pour into the bottle.  I will be ordering another one in a smaller everyday travel size.
_gate_reason: Describes the bottle's size, material, and texture.

title: Nice looking and functional
text: I own 2 of these bottles: the teal/blue 24 oz and the coral/punch 40 oz. I find that I use the 40 oz size most of the time simply because it needs refilling less frequently.  These bottles do their job well. I have only used them for cold water (no hot drinks) and have found them to be very well-insulated, keeping ice for hours at a time even in summer heat. Both bottles came with 2 lids—a screw cap and a sports/straw cap; I’ve not seen either cap style leak. As far as the exterior, the bottle colors are vibrant, and, in my experience, the paint is durable. I would recommend!

title: Quality.
text: I bought this bottle for the main reason of switching from plastic to metal. The quality of thus bottle from the interchangeable caps to the color finish is very high. I use tap water filtered through a Brita filter which is kept in the fridge. When i fill up, the water stays cold for hours and this is even when I'm out and it's left in the car for around 45 mins to an hour. I got a 64oz for work and currently ordered an 18oz for my kid and a 40oz for me to use at home. It really does promote a greater intake of water for me throughout the day. There are days I find myself drinking out 2 refills. This is a great product and some of my colleagues and family members are already interested. Buy this bottle, it's worth the money.

title: Amazing bottles!
text:
Beautiful bottle, and excellent material! I got it to bring with me to college in the coral and pinkish color, and was astounded by the quality! It's made of sustainable metal that does indeed keep it from getting warm when something cold is in it and having condensation when the ice eventually melts!

It comes with a normal cap (not pictured in my review) and the sports cap, which I included a picture of, and two straws! The sports cap is designed for colder liquids, the normal cap for warmer ones - which is super easy to use too!

Recently I brought the bottle with me to a picnic at a park and the ice placed inside early afternoon lasted until well after 7 in the evening, even with changing the water when I finished it! Definitely highly recommend this bottle and seller, so I bought more bottles for my family from them :))

Just to note, it's a bit hard to hold the larger bottles, so they sell handles separately which I've ordered to match each of the bottles I've gotten so far 😄

Worth the money if you want something to keep your drinks cold or hot
_gate_reason: Describes the bottle's colors, materials, and design features.

title: SUPER CUTE and easy to carry
text: Super super super cute, especially with the paracord handle that you can buy separately. I bought a mixed and matched set because I like the colors clashing, but they have colors that match each bottle perfectly. The coral/pink bottle is super bright and pretty, just as the picture shows it. I put ice water in it and an hour or so later the ice was still there, unmelted. I suspect it would stay cool all day. The 64oz bottle is easy to carry from the sports cap so you do NOT have to buy the paracord if you don't want it. I just wanted it to make it slightly easier to carry and also for style. In the picture, I'm holding up a completely full 64oz bottle with the paracord and it didn't slip at all, so it's super durable and stays on well. Highly recommend this bottle (and the paracord, though it's not necessary).
_gate_reason: Describes colors, size, and visual appeal of the bottle and handle.

title: If they get the order right, great bottle.
text:
I purchased this bottle to replace a plastic one in 40 oz. As far as how cold it keeps your water, I have fountain water from over 24 hours ago in it that is roughly the same temperature. It is eerie how cold the water still is.

It offers a steel twist lid and a flip-up straw lid assembly. The straw lid assembly is robust, with a finger loop molded into it, and insulates just as well as the steel lid. I prefer the steel twist lid just because it is easier to drink more water at once. I don't have to explain how straws work.

Why I didn't give this a 5-star rating is because I wanted a 40 oz mandarin orange model and I got a 32 oz teal. My receipt shows a 32 oz black which I would have liked more than teal because teal is nasty but the bottle itself functions perfectly.

The technology they use with the double vacuum also does mean no matter how cold whatever you put in the bottle is the outer temperature will not be reflected by that. The bottle will not get cold to touch. So it's a good bottle if they get the order right.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's colors, sizes, and lid types.

title: Good Looking. Great Performance.
text:
I just picked this up and I'm loving it already. I've had some water-bottle-envy for a little while now. My partner has a 32 oz vacuum flask that's amazing. We've been on a "get healthy" kick and drinking enough water is part of that. My job isn't necessarily conducive to lots of water breaks, but I can bring a bottle with me. I've been buying bottled water. That's fine, but it doesn't stay cold. Enter the Hydro Cell bottle.

Pros:
Looks great - Seriously. The colors pop and the logo is clean. I really like it.
Stainless interior - No smells or flavors stick to the inside of the bottle. I like water. I don't want it to taste like anything else, including plastic or metal.
Powder coated exterior - The powder coat is slip resistant unlike the "raw" stainless steel bottles I see around. Those are slippery as heck.
Strong seal - the cap goes on firmly and doesn't leak. That's a big deal with we're talking about 32 oz. of water.
Wide mouth - I can drop a whole "chain" of ice out of my ice maker in to the bottle. That beats my partner's bottle where we need to put the cubes in individually. Big win here.

Cons:
Uh.... I guess I have to write something here.
The retainer loop may fail at some point. Hydro Cell does have a lifetime warranty. I contacted them during the order process and they were very responsive, so I'm not worried.

Bottom Line:
Highly recommended. This bottle does what it says. It keeps cold stuff cold and hot stuff hot - for a long time.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's colors, materials, and finishes.

title: More worthy of its price than the big brand names
text:
Among large, insulated, cold travel cups with straw lids, I don't think you can beat the value on this one. Just ordered my second one after losing the first. I will continue buying these!

What I love:
- The handle on the cap is oriented to be grabbed from the side, not looped over one finger from above like most. Better for grabbing off my nightstand or out of a cup holder.
- Keeps my drink exactly the temperature I fill it with for hours.
- All components are dishwasher-safe and it doesn't look or taste like anything is getting stuck in the straw cap.
- At least $5 less than more well-known brands across the board
- Durable - After several months of daily use, no marks, scratches or dents on it.
- Pretty colors!

What I don't love:
- 32oz is too wide to fit in any cup holder. Not an issue, but maybe it could be a bit taller and thinner.
- Does not come with pipe cleaners, which personally I need in order to feel like I'm hand washing effectively.
- I think the straw cap requires more effort than other brands to drink from, but not so much that it's uncomfortable.
_gate_reason: The review mentions pretty colors and the cup's size and shape.

title: Excellent bottle, perfect customer service
text:
Not much to say about the bottle other than its flawless. Less than half the price of the other "hydro" brand but virtually indistinguishable. Seriously, I had mine up against my friend's $50 bottle and they're exactly the same in fit, finish, and dimensions. When you look VERY carefully they are slightly different shaped on the bottom, but that's about it.

But what really gets me on here was the customer service. One of the caps came with a slight defect; I reached out and Jordan from hydro cell responded right away. A few days later I received a brand new cap. I am always a bit leery when I order from off brand cut rate products but the quality and customer service is as good, if not better to be honest, than any major brand I've dealt with. If you're looking for a solid product from solid people, this is it.
_gate_reason: The review mentions fit, finish, and dimensions of the bottle.

title: Not worth it

My water is WARM and ice is melted about 1-2hrs after putting ice and cold water in it. Also, after a couple weeks using it, it is INCREDIBLY hard to open the lid now. The rubber ring connected to the cap gets stuck multiple times and I have to adjust it over and over again while opening it. Not easy to do in the car or with one hand. title: High Standard, Great Quality
text:
I've had this bottle now for a couple of months and I'm really impressed with it. I was nervous to make the investment because I didn't realize how much was actually *needed* in a solid bottle- one I can count on for a long time.

I'm thought it was silly at first, that many reviewers commented that it enforces their water drinking and they drink more as a result of ownership. I can attest that it's true! Seeing a bottle I take everywhere, that looks nice, reminds me to drink! My water intake has greatly increased since I've owned it.

It keeps ice forever! It definitely does depend on the size of the cube so I won't stand behind any claim of how long. But I'm shocked at how well it keeps cold and there's been ice in my bottle for 48+ hours.

It does scratch rather easily so I try not to let it roll around and always have the handle attached. I haven't dropped it, so I can't speak on whether or not it dents.

I'm glad I chose to invest in some of the attachments, like the rubber bottom and braided handle. I LOVE the way it looks and I never forget it. I do notice my straw top makes a funny noise (almost like a squeaking when drinking) that I wish it didn't and my bf def knows when I'm drinking from it as a result. (This bottle is literally in my bed at night, all nights). So, that's a bummer but I'm wondering if it's a fluke. I haven't seen anyone else review on the same issue. I noticed, along with other reviwers, that the bottom does tend to slide off occasionally, but I guess, for me, that's a low priority, bcuz I always notice what it looks like without. But that's not to say, I wish it just stay on. And perhaps would benefit from being slightly more fitted. I believe the rubber on has helped some possible damage that may have resulted otherwise so I'm glad to have it.

Grateful for the reviews that really swayed my choice in choosing this specific one bcuz I'm really happy with it.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's appearance and how it looks nice.

title: Works great at first but it won’t last!

The orange color I chose looks great but I have to throw it away because it’s useless. The fliptop leaked but the other lid it came with worked fine. I also didn’t realize that it wouldn’t fit in a standard car cupholder. I definitely won’t buy from this company again and I’m surprised by all the great reviews. I have a feeling that other people are happy at first like me but then don’t bother to update their reviews when the bottle stops working.
_gate_reason: The review describes the orange color of the bottle.

title: Great Bottle... Terrible Lid
text:
I bought this bottle to replace a decade old Nalgene that has served me well for literally that entire ten years. The idea of a metal water bottle that holds its temp was quite alluring so I figured this one would be good because of the reviews. Because of the nature of the issue I have shown in the photos, I will talk about both the water bottle itself and then the cap (lid).

The Bottle - 5 STARS - Holds the temperature of whatever you put in it for a very long time. I put ice with water in and leave it in a hot car for 6 hours, it still has ice when I drink it. Pretty amazed by this. Its really durable and I have yet to dent or scratch it. I really do love this bottle.

The Lid - 1 STAR - As you can see by the photos, the cheap plastic they used for the lid leash (? or whatever it is called that keeps the lid on the bottle when you unscrew it) is just cracking and becoming rather hazardous. It has produced two VERY SHARP points that are rigid and will poke / cut you easily. To note - I do not carry the bottle by this leash, I have a Jug-Lug that I use and makes it easier to carry anyways. I carried my Nalgene by the leash for a decade and it is still in decent shape. I've only had this bottle for like 7 months and it is falling apart.

So what do I do from here? Cut the leash and have a lid and a bottle? Until I lose the lid I suppose that may work.

Here is the deal HydroCell, go ask Nalgene for some advice on polymer production if you are going to have plastic for this product. Their's is durable and seems to work just fine.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's durability and the lid's cracking appearance.

title: Awesome water bottle w/ outstanding customer service

title: Game changer: water bottle edition
text:
I received my Hydro Cell water bottle in record time, and immediately couldn't wait to put it to the test. I have an RTIC that I love, but it's not very functional as an athletic water bottle and us more for sitting events. I needed something I could easily take with me for outdoor exercise, that would keep me hydrated and cool.

My thoughts while unboxing:

- It was packaged well, there would be no chance of damage. It was in a box, inside of the shipping box. Very simple packaging, but strong and impressionable.

- The bottle is very large. It does fit in my tiny hands, but I'll get a lot of fluid out of this. The screw top attaches firmly with no chance of leak if it tips over. I turned it upside down (when full) for a minute and no water escaped. The screw top is made of a rubber-looking material with grooves in it for an easier twist. There is a loop that you can hold onto that feels sturdy.

- The look itself is bold and smooth. I'm hoping the outer part of it that is powder coated, which is rather thick for insulation, does not sweat with temperature changes, which is something I put to the test on arrival. The bottle does feel sturdy and not too slick that it will slide out if your hands.

I filled it with ice water at 3 pm, and will be checking over the rest of the day for how well the water stats cold, if the bottle sweats, and any other important things to note. Meanwhile, I have attached a few photos.

After an hour and 15 minutes, my ice had already melted but the water was icy cold. Another hour and 15 minutes and the water is the same icy temperature. A total of 4 hours later, and the water still tastes like it did in the beginning. It's very clear that even though the ice melted, the temperature is holding very well. Writing this update at 9 hours later, water is still cold/temperature unchanged and there is no sweating on my bottle. I am so impressed.

I truly, honestly can't think of any cons. This is a game changer for my workouts.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's size, color, and material details.

title: Love my hydro cell!!!

title: Beautiful, Durable and Recommended
text: I love this water bottle. My husband bought it for me for Christmas and as devoted as I am to my yeti because it keeps my water cold forever, I thought I should give this a try. It comes with a screw on lid with two straw attachments, a screw on lid without a straw for hot drink options and is easy to switch out. I never use the hot option so I can't say to how long or well it keeps drinks warm. I use it specifically for water and I fill it with ice in the morning, add water thru the day and never add more ice. It keeps all day, my water remains so cold and I find it much nicer than my yeti for all day carrying around. The straw lid closes tightly and I have never had an issue with it leaking. It is beautiful, I have the purple and the color is beautiful and retains it even after washing multiple times. My favorite part is how well made it is. I've dropped this off my balcony, on the concrete multiple times and it's slid off my car after I drove off and forgot I set it on the roof. Even with all the drops and abuse, it doesn't have a scratch or dent one. It still looks brand new and that is impressive. I carry this with me everywhere now and I can't recommend this water bottle enough. It replaced my yeti and down the road when I want a replacement, I plan on buying another one of these in a different color. High praise for this water bottle from me!!!

title: ONE drop and it broke the strap, and dented.
text:
Update:
I'm bumping it up a star for the great customer service. I contacted them and was immediately shipped out a brand new cap + (including the strap). Thanks Guys!

Previous Review:
I had the bottle for maybe 3 days, took it out for my first bike ride, put it into the bike's bottle holder, started riding along a half concrete, half sand beach bike path, and boom, 5 minutes into the ride I hit a patch of sand and the bottle fell out of the holder onto the concrete only about a foot from the ground and at a speed of no more than .3 mph. For some reason it sounded like a minor bomb popped, since everyone within a radius of 20 feet immediately turned and watched me to see wth just made that noise.

I pick the bottle up and it already has a small dent in it (see pic), ok, no big deal, that might be expected and it doesn't affect the temperature, but then I also notice the strap that holds the cap on is broken. Half torn. What? How in the HELL does that happen on a foot drop?

You'd think when you pay a premium for a high quality bottle like this, they'd be able to make a rubber strap that won't literally *break*. SMH. SO now after only having the bottle for 3 days I have to live with it with a broken strap.

NOT happy about that.

As far as the usual review goes, the bottle itself does seem well made and high quality in every other area but that strap, and it really does keep drinks cold for a long time. I filled it with 3/4 ice and the rest water around 5pm one day, took it out on a 90° day, took a few sips and then forgot about it in the back seat of my car the entire next day. Opened it up around 11pm the next night and there was still ice in it, and the water was ice cold. And sitting in my car in 80-90 degree temps that whole day ... that's really impressive.

I haven't had a chance to try how it handles hot drinks yet.

One minor gripe; yes the outside has a bit of a texture to make it "easier" to hold w/out slipping, but, I think it could be even better. It's still slightly slippery and I could easily see dropping it if not getting a good enough grip. I'd like it to be to the point where I can barely hold it and it feels like there's no way it's going to slip out of my hand. Maybe rubberize it with a really grippy rubber, or something. Maybe it's just more of a problem with the big bottle and it's weight when full, I bought the 40oz bottle.

As far as it's looks go, I looks cool enough. Looks to me kind of like a pipe bomb or something, maybe that's another reason people kept watching me after they heard it drop.

All in all, a pretty nice bottle, a bit more expensive than it should be, and they NEED to replace that strap with one that isn't so fragile.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's appearance and texture, including its cool look.

title: Found a water bottle that I love after trying different brands.
text: I really love this water bottle. I'm so glad I gave it a try. And the surprising thing is, it's more affordable than other brands. I have tried the Iron Flask, but returned it because the straw was too short and the straw lid mouth piece was a bit cumbersome and some air would come in the mouth when drinking the water (just being fair, the narrow mouth straw lid of the Iron Flask was small and great though); also that brand was more expensive too. I also tried the Simple Modern, but if you shake it around it will leak slightly from the straw lid, and the lid handle has some sharp edges uncomfortable for the fingers when you hold. I'm very happy with these Hydrocell water bottles. And the customer service is excellent, they are sending me extra straws because one of the water bottles I bought came missing the extra straw and it's always good to have extra. Just so you know, if the water is difficult to come out, requiring too much suction, the straw might be a tad bit too long, this happened with my 64 oz, so I just cut off with scissors a tiny little bit of the straw in a very slight angle. By the way, I also have a small Hydro Flask that I bought many years ago and today they are very expensive, don't be fooled by the logo, the Hydrocell is just as good.

title: The BEST choice in any size
text:
I just bought this, my second HydroCell water bottle - this one a 30 oz. - because my first (20 oz) was the object of such envy. Gifted this to my hubbie, so now my husband has his own HydroCell and can keep his own drinks cold or hot without borrowing mine. ;) The HydroCell keeps stuff cold for at least 12-15 hours (my ice cubes are still there). It'll keep things hot for a good 8 hours. Part of the duration depends on where/how the bottle is kept. In hot sun inside the car, the cold time might be a little shorter; in a freezing cold car, the hot time might change a bit. Overall, though, these bottles WORK for a LONG time.

The bottle itself is GREAT. I like the wide mouth because it's easy to throw in ice cubes, or to fill it with whatever you might like (sorbet!). HydroCell sends you two options for lids: a standard metal, flat lid, and a BPA-free plastic lid with a flip top straw. Both come included; you don't need to buy a second lid (for more money). The metal then is 100% leak proof. In my experience, the flip top lid has been leak proof as well. The fliptop 'could' leak as there is a tiny vent hole (so the straw works despite the seal), and because the flip top is a moving part with attendant gaps. It MIGHT leak, but so far I haven't had the problem in the 2+ years I've used the 20 oz. bottle.

I chose these HydroCell bottles over the (admittedly hip and "in") HydroFlask because of the BIG price difference, and the fact that HydroCell gives you the two tops. My son opted for a HydroFlask; he's at the age that brand matters. Although we bought our bottles within days of each other, my HydroCell still looks new (despite multiple drops & bangs). His HydroFlask is dented and the finish is peeling off at the top and bottom. It's possible he's harder on his bottle, but I think that my HydroCell has a better/tougher powder coat finish.

The 20 oz. size fits in car cup holders and bike bottle holders. The larger diameter of the 30 oz. size does not. If you add a silicone bottle boot (which I did on both) the 20 oz. is a quite tight fit in the cup holder. The 30 still won't fit ;) .

Clean-up is super easy for these: just wash it out with soap and water. Ease of washing is definitely helped by the wide mouth.

I recommend these HydroCell bottles to everyone. They're very well made and an excellent deal.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's colors, materials, size, and finishes.

title: Temperature Control, Sturdy, Great Customer Service
text:
I have had this water bottle for over two years and I love it. I have carried this water bottle around with my EVERYWHERE. I like it so much that I bought one for my husband and best friend. You only want the best for those close to you, so you already know how good a product is if people are buying them for close ones. I was curious how there could be one star reviews on this, so when I looked, I saw it was mostly due to the non-steel lid and ice melting. I don't use the non-steel lid with the straw (I thoroughly enjoy having my lid strapped to my bottle, and being able to hold on to it, so I only use the steel lid with the rubber part that connects to my water bottle. Plus, I am thankful for the steel lid because I try not to drink out of plastic. Thus, I cannot speak to the other lid if that is something you are looking for.). Secondly, in terms of ice melting - yes, it does melt. HOWEVER, your water is kept cold for hours (I have never had it where I had ice/my water at a cool temperature and it EVER warming up.)!

This is a great water bottle, and it is at a price point where you feel comfortable in investing in it. Having it for 2+ years, and filling it up 2-3 times a day (32 ounces), I have received AT LEAST 1,825 uses out of it (2 years X 2.5 fill-ups on average). There is great temperature control, it is sturdy (this is coming from someone who is clumsy), and it has a great design (I have purchased the teal 32 oz, black/white ombre 32 oz, and navy 40 oz for myself and others.)! I use the teal one, and I LOVE the color - it is so vibrant and a beautiful color). Also, my husband and I have both put stickers on ours and they work well. My husband has the black/white ombre one, and has a white sticker that overlaps both, and it looks so sleek! The only thing about my water bottle is that it is too wide for my cup holders in my car since I don't have large/adjustable cup holders. However, as long as the lid is screwed on tightly, I put it on the ground and it does not leak.

I also want to give a shout out to the customer service team; if you have any questions or concerns about your water bottle, just go to their contact page on their website and send them an e-mail. They will get back to you within 24 hours and help you solve your issue if it falls under their lifetime warranty - how cool is that? You don't get that very often with companies, and that is something to really considering when buying any product you plan on using consistently. Most don't offer superior service, but you are lucky if you buy from Hydro Cell because you will receive awesome customer service that is responsive and respectful! Special shout out to Jordan at Hyrdo Cell for assisting me!
_gate_reason: The review describes the colors and design of the water bottles.

title: Hydro Cell  vs Hydro Flask
text:
It was a choice between this and the Hydro Flask. Liked the fact that this came with two tops and the review about their customer service. Read one review that said the straw top leaked. There's a vent in the top so you can use the straw. If you close it when you're done no leaking at all.
As for the performance, I got my wife a Hydro Flask because if it's a big name it must be better right?. It's not. The Hydro Cell keeps ice just as long as the Hydro Flask. There's virtually no difference and the performance of both are beyond amazing. I can put iced tea in my Hydro Cell on Monday morning and on Tuesday morning it is still ice cold. Minimal melting. Supposedly with the straw top it doesn't keep it as cold as the regular cap because it's not as well insulated. I haven't even tried the regular cap yet but if that's true it can keep things ice cold for days. It's mind boggling how well this works.
One caveat is the 20 oz. size, while it fits a cup holder perfectly, isn't as easy to get ice into as the larger sizes. So when I use my fridge ice maker I kind of have to keep my hands around the mouth to guide the ice in. I could crush it and there would't be any issues but I think the cubes work better.
Over all, Hydro Cell vs. Hydro Flask. Both are amazing. With Hydro Cell, you get some really cool color choices, both styles of caps, and save a ton of money. My 20 oz Hydro Cell was $18 vs. Hydro Flask at $38. Every once and a while you find a great product that's a real deal on Amazon and this Hydro Cell is one of them. Can't say enough good things about it.
_gate_reason: The review mentions cool color choices and the 20 oz size.

Keeps it cool and isn’t heavy. Easy to carry handles on both lid options. It’s not slippery in your hand and is surprisingly comfortable to hold without or without the handle. You can choose to have the stainless steel flow cap or the black straw-attached cap. Straw is nice and sturdy and gives a pretty decent outflow, not just a little sip, so very refreshing each time I take a drink.
Easy to disassemble and clean each piece, Yes even by hand (not dishwasher safe)
Throw a few ice cubes in and it Keeps my water cold well over 12 hours. I didn’t want to get into the “hydro flask” band wagon but I’m glad I did
Will probably end up ordering another

title: Holds temp and doesnt leak while flying
text: This water bottle is kinda legit. It def keeps water cold for hours at a time. I havent had the chance to test it for longer than 8 hours at a time yet but so far the water is just as cold with no temp drop. I have been using the straw lid and it works super well. There isnt any gurgling sound when u drink. And the straw goes all the way to the bottom of the cup. The paint does scuff a bit from normal usage. Not from just handling it but when it gets bumped around in a bag and in the car and such. But nothing that bad. It does get pretty heavy when filled. So not really ideal to carry all the time. (I have the 40 ounce) I also use this while flying. I wanted a bottle that would hold up to pressure changes at dif altitudes. This one is great. The straw doesnt leak at all unless u open it at a high altitude then all the pressure releases and it will kinda explode a bit of water. But just leaving the straw closed it is cool. I fly my own plane and do not pressurize the cabin so if you fly commercially then it really wouldnt be an issue at all. Overall im super happy with my purchase. But im kinda having buyers remorse on the color I chose. Its too bright. I should have gone with a more chill color. But o well

title: Ice still frozen overnight (14+ hours) ❄️
text:
I was tasked with ordering ten of these for our maintenance crew and office staff to reduce the amount of plastic from bottled water we consume each month. We handed them out yesterday and so far everyone is pleased with everything from the colors and finish to the ability to keep the water cold.

These are very well constructed and they work as advertised, so far. I fell asleep early last night and when I went to clean it after a long, hot day yesterday (90°F outdoors and hotter in my truck) it still had ice. Not slushy little bits but solid pieces from our ice maker that does chipped ice.

The only thing that's been a negative is the straw. The ladies in the office complained that it's easier to ditch the straw and drink by tipping it and letting it come out the mouthpiece.

We got a mix of 40 oz and 24 oz. My ratings are for both sizes because the construction is the same.

One piece of advice. You don't have to fill it with ice. I got too much ice this morning (it's supposed to be in the 90s again today) and it hasn't melted fast enough to keep up with my thirst. Not a big deal but you definitely won't need as much ice as for other products. That's a good thing - more water or beverage, less ice.

Because of my positive experience with Hydrocell bottles and its Customer Service staff I will be buying a few more bottles for my friends just to support the company because I think more companies should follow in there direction in taking care if its customers and building great products.
Thanks Hydrocell.

title: LOVE this water bottle
text:
I'm really not a water drinker, I've never enjoyed drinking water, and couldn't drink it at all unless it was ice cold. Now I'm pregnant so I really have to be drinking water constantly. This bottle is perfect, and super cute too. I prefer the straw top, and this came with both so I could change it if I wanted. It says cold for SO long (holds the ice that I put in it), which is great for water drinking when I wake up in the middle of the night. I can't recommend this bottle enough. And the price is amazing for what you're getting.
I originally bought a large plastic water bottle, but the rubbery taste would not go away no matter how much I washed it. The water doesn't taste metalic at all, which is something I was worried about since I'd never had a metal bottle before. The only downside is that if you aren't careful, it can get some dings and dents. Also not spill proof, but I've knocked it over in the car and it barely leaked at all.
All in all, I love this bottle and it's helped me drink more water since I bought it than I ever have before.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the bottle's cute design and material qualities.

title: Amazing water bottle and even better customer service!
text:
I received my first bottle (32 oz Fuscia bottle) about a month or so back and am EXTREMELY happy with it. It keeps my water cold for SUCH a long time! My Fiance and I both use HydroCell bottles and we LOVE them!

Unfortunately, about a week or so back, I dropped my bottle and it dented :-( I was bummed and let the company know and they responded quickly letting me know that that will happen if it hits a hard surface because although the materials they use are high quality, if it was thick enough not to dent, I would not be able to carry it, let alone use it. Jordan was very understanding though and knew that I was upset because my bottle was newer and kindly offered me a replacement. This my friends is a great example of customer service at its finest, something that I definitely do not see a lot of anymore.

Thank you very much Jordan and the team at HydroCell - I am a customer for life! :-)
_gate_reason: The review describes the color and size of the water bottle.

At first, it took a lot of effort to drink through the straw and now, just about a month after getting it, it's hit or miss whether or not any water will come up the straw anyway. I have replaced the straw and still have the issue leading me to believe that perhaps the straw cap is flawed? Could I drink out of the wide mouth lid? Totally. But I bought this bottle specifically for the straw. I am having a difficult time contacting the seller as well which is raising additionally redflags. It does seem like it could be a great product and perhaps I got a lemon. I would love to be proven wrong but, for now, I can add this bottle to the garbage pile.

title: Keeps Water COLD!!
text: I have 3 of these plus I've bought one for a friend. I'm ordering another because I want 2 for cold drinks and 2 for hot drinks. I can fill one of these 32 oz bottles with 8 ice cubes and cold water before I go to bed at 9:00 pm and there will still be ice cubes keeping that water cold when I get up at 8:30 the next morning unless I drink all the water during the night. If I do, the ice will still be there in the morning. Big enough mouth for any size ice cubes. I've dropped them, dented them, chipped the powder coating and they STILL work!! I just wish they were easier to clean. I've ordered a tool that was made specifically for cleaning the straw lids for these bottles so that's going to make cleaning easier. I've ordered vacuum sealed bottles from at least 4 other companies and not one of them could hold a candle to the Hydro Cell, especially for the price! Plus they come in a rainbow of colors so you're sure to find one that catches your eye and that will be something you enjoy so you'll stay hydrated. I fill mine about 4x a day and my kidneys are happier than ever - a good thing for a diabetic with beginning stages of kidney disease. Just the increased water intake alone has brought my kidney function closer to "normal". Oh - and because of the special powder coating (keep it OUT of the dishwasher!!) they don't "sweat." So no mess! They don't leak, either, and mine stays on my bed beside me when I'm sleeping so it's on its side. Great any time of year whether you prefer hot or cold drinks (or even soup)! I'm going to test and see if ice cream shakes do as well in these bottles, as I suspect they will. Enjoy!!

title: If you're weighing your options, this is the one.
text:
I love this water bottle. It's exactly what it claims to be. Lightweight, durable, easy to hold and carry, easy to drink from, zero leaks, zero complaints. Detailed review:
I got a 40oz bottle that came with a metal screw on cap, a plastic sports cap, and 2 straws. For all temperature and leak claims, the company uses the screw on cap as its point of reference. I use the sports cap almost exclusively, which comes with a warning that it MAY possibly leak due to its moving parts. I have never had that happen. My water still tastes and feels like it just came out of the fridge by the time I get off work 12 hours later. The straws are high quality and reach the bottom of the bottle. I cleaned every component before using the bottle, which was not difficult at all. The inner threads on both caps require a little more attention than your standard water bottle but the 15 extra seconds of cleaning are a great tradeoff for zero leaks and amazing temperature retention. You will need a straw brush to clean the straws and the inside of the sports cap mouthpiece. The "grip tech" coating sounded a little ridiculous at first but they weren't kidding. It is virtually impossible to drop this bottle. The extra grip also makes it easier to unscrew, which is a nice accessibility feature, intentional or otherwise. And if you did drop the bottle, it would be completely fine. I throw this bottle around like nothing else. I trust it completely. Plus, the colors are vibrant and accurate to the pictures shown on the product listing. I've tried similar products from other brands, and they don't even compare. I was actually looking for a yeti water bottle but this is better, and cheaper. The 40oz is the perfect size, shaped like a normal bottle instead of a jug but with that extra bit of height so it doesn't have to be filled quite as often. I really can't praise it enough. I love this water bottle and I highly recommend it to anyone who drinks water.
_gate_reason: The review describes the bottle's colors, size, and shape in detail.

title: Keeps water super cold! And 5 stars for amazing customer service!!
text:
I love this water bottle - bought it to take on a trip to Costa Rica last year and it worked great! Filled with water and a few ice cubes, and the water stayed cold all day, even in 90 degree weather. Color is great and the paint is very durable - it is gone on camping trips, plane trips and I take it in my backpack on hiking trips nearly every week. The only drawback is that the 32 oz size weighs 3 lbs, a lot of weight to add to my pack, but worth it to keep the water nice and cold. Sadly, after just 10 months, the plastic strap that holds the metal cap onto the bottle is starting to crack - I’m hoping Hydro Cell will replace it.

*Update - I contacted Hydro Cell through their website contact form, and someone got back to me the very next morning. I explained the problem with the strap, and they immediately shipped a new cap free of charge, which I received in 2 days. Really nice product and A+ customer service!!
_gate_reason: The review describes the color and durability of the water bottle's paint.

title: Easy to drink from
text:
UPDATE: After I wrote this review, I was contacted by the seller and they sent a replacement bottle. I told them what I did to keep the bottle clean and how I purged it as well. They were easy to work with and to talk with.. We both agreed that what was done to clean it was the correct way. After washing the new bottle today, there was an improvement. I filled up the bottle with water and I did not smell or taste the soap I used to clean the first one. (Just to test I again washed the first one with the same soap and I could still smell and taste the soap again. The first bottle might of been a lemon.) So I can now fully recommend this bottle. Thank you for getting in touch with me and working so well with me.

TL;DR: Both tops and bottle seems to keep soap and left over water residue in or at the top of the bottle making drinking anything taste and smell horrible. Keep top and inside clean often and wipe off the top after each drink.
Long review:
I bought this couple of weeks ago. When I got it I did wash this out. Hot water, soap and, what I thought, throughly cleaned and even dried this out. Later in the day both the straw top, straw and the inside of the bottle smelled and tasted like soap I used to clean it with was still there. I of course stopped drinking from this the same day and washed, rinsed and dried again the same night. It was better after washing ans rinsing for a while (15 minutes to make sure the soap was gone.) The next morning when I went to take a drink from this, it smelled bad. Like it had kept the water in there for a while. It had only been ONE NIGHT. I then again washed it out and wiped inside and out making sure there was no water residue anywhere. Again it smelled fine. I put just water in there for work and about 6 pm when I opened the top to take a drink. It continued to smell horrible. There was nothing but water in it this time. No ice, it wasn't jostled around. The water came from a purified bottle of water. I do not understand why it would keep hold of soap and make water smell and taste like it's been there for weeks when you just put it in there hours ago. I have many stainless steel water bottles and none of them kept hold of soap and water residue after washing, rinsing and drying by hand. The only one that kept doing this was my Yeti 40 Oz straw top. After I got straws that would reach the bottom it was completely fine. So the straw was the problem. But this one the whole inside and both tops hold these residues. It's very cute and the silicone bottom that you can get with this is great. It has saved me from many of would be spills.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the bottle's cute appearance and silicone bottom.

title: Attached lid and impressive insulation
text:
This insulation is impressive.. I usually fill water bottles, even insulated ones, the very top with ice before adding water. After filling about half up with water, the ice settles and I cram some more in. The ice in other bottles melts as you go and you always have cold water. You can't do that with this. You will run out of water before enough ice melts, even if it's sitting in the sun for many hours on a very hot day. Go figure. Don't fill it more than half full of ice unless you have a second bottle of water to pour in when you run out and only have ice left.
Lots of insulated bottles are similar but this one also has an attached lid which seems to be rare. Lots of color choices and sizes as well, plus it was one of the most affordable ones I found.
It also fits nicely in a car cup holder.
_gate_reason: Describes color choices, sizes, and the attached lid feature of the product.

title: Perfect!
text:
I bring this bottle to work with me every day! I will admit, the size intimidated many of my coworkers at first... but I find that most days I drink 80-90% of the water in it.

Just a warning: it is HEAVY when full of liquid. This is a SOLID waterbottle.

I only use it for ice water, so cleaning the actual bottle isn't a daily thing. On the weekends I pop in a bottle cleaning tab. Easy.

The bottle keeps your stuff COLD. It would sit outside for an hour or more when I was working outside and even when the black top was very hot in the summer sun, the water inside was still cold with some ice!

I got the lilac and white one and the color is just so soft and relaxing. It isn't super abnoxious or anything, even with its size.

There are ups and downs to having a straw instead of a chug top, but with a bottle this heavy a chug top might have made drinking from it a little difficult. Besides, this straw has a pretty good ability to get practically all of the water out!

Recommended!!!
_gate_reason: The review describes the colors and size of the water bottle.

title: heck yeah this water bottle is great
text: This water bottle is actually really great! Ok, my first of these bottles was the 18 oz. white/black ombre, and it lasted me over a year and a half. This is kinda remarkable because this was my only water bottle and I used it pretty much every day- took it to school for a while, came with me between my two jobs, got dropped a lot, and survived many trips through the dishwasher (which you aren't supposed to do and explains a lot.) The only issue I had with it was that the black hard plastic on the metal cap ripped after about a year (mostly the dishwasher's fault,) and the little plastic tab that you use on the other cap to pull up the straw broke around the same time which made it slightly harder to use but honestly wasn't a big deal (like I said, this guy got dropped a lot and held up to a lot of abuse.) But yeah, decided that it was time for a new one- here's a side-by-side comparison. Honestly, I'm still going to use the old one for the container and switch the caps! If you're looking for a solid water or hot drink container, this is a really good one, especially for the price. Would recommend!
_gate_reason: Describes the colors and materials of the water bottle.

title: Can work in a bike bottle cage!
text: I got several of the 24oz size bottles hoping I could make them work in the standard-sized aluminum bottle cages on our bikes.  They originally fit if you really pushed the bottles in--not ideal, and I know others mentioned that sort of thing scratching the bottles (I also noticed a small scratch when I tried this).  So I carefully stretched each of the cages until the bottles fit more easily, and I cut small pieces of felt (like what you buy to put on furniture feet so they don't scratch your floors) to stick on the inside of the cage to prevent scratching.  A bit of effort just to make a bottle fit?  Yeah.  But I couldn't find an insulated steel water bottle that would fit without some kind of adjustments, and I really liked these bottles anyway.  In the end, worth it.  The bottles look great, are easy to clean, and keep our drinks cold while we ride.  Would purchase again.
_gate_reason: The bottles look great and are easy to clean.

title: Well made for the money.
text:
Pros: Nice wide mouth makes adding ice easy. Doesn't build up condensation around the lid like my old Contigo. Finish has a nice textured feel to it so its not slippery in your hand. Water stays very cold and ice barely melts over night. Simple and durable lid designs.

Cons: The screw on lids loop is quite firm plastic would prefer a softer rubber. It makes it awkward to drink as you have to move it out of the way and hold it as it keeps its shape. A rubber buttom would be nice to avoid denting wood tables and slipping on smooth top office desks.

Overall this is much better than the comparable Contigo with their gimmicky lids that spring mechanisms always break after a month or two.
Would definitely buy again as this is extremely well made for the price and includes two lids.
_gate_reason: Describes the textured finish and wide mouth of the product.

title: Best cup to drink cold beverages out of when wearing a mask!
text: This very sturdy, stainless steel bottle comes with two lids, one standard lid, creating a vacuum seal like a thermos, keeping beverages at temperature through to the next day and a sport cap lid with a flip up drinking spout attached to a straw. When I use the sport cap, I can simply flip up the spout and slip it under my mask to drink cold water, no need to take off any part of my mask or tilt my head back, and flip the spout back down so nothing is sticking out and have not had any leaking problems, even when it was rolling around in the car! There is also no outer condensation so can keep it on my desk without worrying about spills or puddles. I dropped it on pavement and it now has a small dent on the bottom, but they do sell a protective, silicone boot as well as other accessories like straps and replacement lids. It only came with one removable silicone straw, but I have not had to replace it after several months of constant use, no leakage whatsoever. I did need to buy a long straw brush to thoroughly clean the spout and long straw. The instructions are very clear on the best way to clean all the pieces and remove tea/coffee stains and it has not imparted any funky smells or taste to my beverages, but I do keep one bottle only for water and use the other for hot beverages or iced tea. It is a bit heavy, but I have the large, 40oz bottle.  I love the colors and slip proof, matte finish!
_gate_reason: The review describes colors and a slip proof, matte finish.

title: Pretty good
text: We bought 6 of these (4 14oz, 2 24oz) as stocking stuffers.  The kids loved the colors we got (purple/white, pink, red/yellow, navy). All bottles came with straw lids and straws (some came with 2 straws). The bottles do leak a little out of the straw lid when tipped because water is coming back out the straw. We also did a drive over a mountain pass and my bottle leaked some out of the straw and filled up the cup holder. It only happened on mine so not sure if it was a Venturi effect as the pressure changed or what, didn’t happen on the way home. Haven’t used the non-straw lids to know if those leak. We have a 2013 Sienna and the bottles fit perfectly in the cup holders.  Haven’t put anything hot in them, but they seem to keep cold stuff cold. However it is winter so I guess we’ll see during summer time.
_gate_reason: The review describes the colors and sizes of the bottles.

title: Amazing bottle, Amazing Customer Service
text: As an ICU nurse, I don’t stop much at work these days so a water bottle is a must to stay alive. I bought this because I liked the colors (the hombre orange is simply amazing). But then as I’ve used it the past 3 months, it has become a part of me. My wife says I’m “rough on equipment” but in reality, this bottle has taken 15-20 falls from 5-6 ft and barely has any visible damage. IT IS DURABLE. I continue to try to find an excuse to buy another but haven’t found one yet. Unfortunately the plastic sport cap gets a lil dinged up when it meets concrete from 5 feet up falling from my backpack, but it’s plastic so... to be expected. That being said, I ordered replacement lids from the company and the service is AMAZING. I’ve never experienced anything like it! The only con I’ve found is plainly spelled out in their listing, the sport cap isn’t 100% waterproof and if you leave it upside down in a backpack an oz or so will leak out over 24 hours. Don’t hesitate, buy this!
_gate_reason: The review describes the colors and durability of the water bottle.

title: Keeping the kidneys Happy!!!
text:
Welp, I've been whining about not liking lukewarm water long enough - after several years of my doctors' warnings about dehydration & forth-coming kidney issues, hubby came out with the one remark that hit home..."It's alot easier to drink water than take meds...!". Whoa! & he's someone who Knows.
So. Research & taa-daa, the Hydro Cell comes up first in my feed.
Reviews; decent. Price point; decent. Even pretty colors!! Done.
Am I happy? Sure am! This baby keeps water at ice-cold Drinkable temps for Hours! Comes with a straw adaptor or a screw on top.
No off plastic flavors - the stainless steel innards are so easy to clean.
It's tough, too - I've dropped it on pavement & banged it around a little but still looks new.
The only thing you need to realize is that stainless steel has weight & waterfilled stainless steel has More weight. The little loopy handle works okay but it's kinda awkward to get used to.
Oh...& when you drink alot of water, there's pee!! Who Knew!!!
_gate_reason: The review mentions pretty colors and describes the stainless steel material.
